Partner-sight noted on the $6,068.91 amount and Daniel's proposed accounting path.

What stands out to me is not just the duplicate posting but the vendor-management gap behind the May 22 manual re-key. If a renewal can be manually re-entered without clear confirmation of the active subscription footprint, that points to a broader controls concern around how vendor changes are being validated before they hit the books.

No change from my side on the accounting mechanics Daniel approved. Please keep the exception in investigating until the correction is prepared and the side-workstream is closed through the IT/CIO check.

Use FP-2026-05 and book the correction as DR Accounts Payable - External Vendors 210000 and CR Prepaid Software Subscriptions 131000 for $6,068.91. This should reverse the May 22 manual re-key rather than touch the May 15 auto-renewal entry.

Before I move this toward disposition today, three unresolved items are still open: Daniel needs to name the approver, Andrea needs partner-sight on the material amount, and Ben needs to confirm the schedule mechanics impacted by the duplicate subscription posting.\", \"user\": \"persona_010\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1780081080.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_022_orphan_exception_emily_opens_close_channel_thread_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780075500.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I can take approver on this one. Jones is right that the urgency-upgrade auto-assign rule misfired, which is why the approver field stayed blank when exc_4d5d3582698946 escalated. From my side, the May 22 manual re-key looks like a vendor-management process gap rather than a new consumption event. Before we close the loop, IT/CIO needs to confirm there should only be one active subscription seat-count in force going forward so we reverse the duplicate cleanly and keep the prepaid schedule aligned.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780069680.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_022_orphan_exception_daniel_names_himself_and_flags_vendor_angle_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780081080.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"From the prepaid schedule side, the duplicate posting created two parallel 12-month amortization plans for the same annual subscription amount: one tied to the May 15 auto-renewal and one tied to the May 22 manual entry. I want to tighten up our accounts payable workflow for managed clients so we are handling fewer exceptions by email and less one-off cleanup. At the moment, the process feels a little different depending on who is on the file, which makes review and handoff harder than it needs to be.

My thought is to keep the intake consistent: vendor setup or updates, approver matrix, payment timing, and any special handling notes all captured in one place. From there, we can standardize the review step so there is a clear checkpoint before anything is approved or released.

If you are open to it, I would like to put together a simple workflow outline and have you sanity-check the client-facing pieces, especially the intake fields and how we route approvals across the team.

Thanks,

input.command:
F=/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/7129832f-f41e-4440-b6fd-4ed04c5416d9/tool-results/mcp-mcp_3ffecdca-oracle_gl_list_journal_entries-1781734781881.txt
python3 - "$F" <<'EOF'
import json,sys
d=json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))
r=json.loads(d["result"])
jes=r.get("journal_entries") or r.get("entries") or []
print("total JEs:", r.get("total_journal_entries"), "returned:", len(jes))
for je in jes:
    desc=(je.get("description") or "")
    lines=je.get("lines",[])
    accts=[l.get("gl_account_number") for l in lines]
    # flag entries touching legal/accrual accounts or mentioning counsel
    txt=json.dumps(je).lower()
    if any(k in txt for k in ["accru","counsel","lattimer","legal","52185","52,185","12400","12,400","7500","6068","kestrel","520000","531","220","221","230"]) or "530000" in accts:
        print("----", je.get("id"), je.get("status"), je.get("entry_type"), "| ", desc)
        for l in lines:
            print("     ", l.get("gl_account_number"), l.get("debit"), l.get("credit"), "|", (l.get("memo") or "")[:70])
EOF

[{"id": "issue_05de9a8fc05c4effb88c2797c882ce41", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": null, "state_id": "todo", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Review CrownPeak AP queue and tune duplicate detection for memo variance", "description": "Trigger invoice for northstar_legal: apinv_e0235997b4964a8f, CrownPeak Cyber Defense invoice VEN-030-353041 for $459.84, has been sitting pending approval since 2026-03-09 (83 days old as of the weekly exceptions scan) and appears to be a duplicate of already paid sibling invoice VEN-030-353009, paid on 2026-03-13 for the same March 2026 MSSP cycle. The sibling was paid four days after VEN-030-353041 was issued, indicating CrownPeak AR caught the duplicate but did not void the reissue cleanly on their side.

Vendor-side confirmation relayed internally indicates VEN-030-353041 was a billing-system reissue generated during CrownPeak's February 2026 platform migration (roughly three weeks before the duplicate was issued) and VEN-030-353009 was the valid invoice. No additional March billing is expected.

Control gap observed: duplicate detection did not catch the sibling invoices because memo text differed materially enough to evade the current match logic. One memo reads "Mar MSSP retainer" and the other reads "March MSSP monthly fee." This should be reviewed as a workflow/control issue, not only a one-off duplicate dismissal. Tariq's parallel-history pull on the CrownPeak vendor file backs up the duplicate finding.

Also review the two other long-pending CrownPeak items in the northstar_legal AP queue called out in the weekly exceptions review. Brenda's quick pass suggests they do not look like obvious duplicates, so they need separate aging and approval-path review.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document the duplicate relationship between apinv_e0235997b4964a8f / VEN-030-353041 and paid sibling VEN-030-353009.
- Propose a duplicate-detection rule adjustment or fuzzy-match threshold change for memo-variance cases like "Mar MSSP retainer" vs "March MSSP monthly fee" for Priya to sign off on as rule owner.
- Review the two other long-pending CrownPeak invoices for northstar_legal and summarize whether they are approval bottlenecks, coding issues, or separate vendor follow-ups.
- Capture recommended AP queue cleanup steps for CrownPeak items so the same pattern is easier to detect going forward.

Related context from internal escalation is preserved in email thread email_scen_033_ap_escalation_0003 and vendor confirmation email_scen_033_ap_escalation_0004.", "priority": 2, "estimate": 3, "due_date": "2026-06-02", "labels": ["ap-controls", "duplicate-detection", "vendor-review", "northstar_legal", "crownpeak"], "links": [], "is_archived": false, "created_at": "2026-05-20T13:23:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-05-27T18:44:07Z"}, {"id": "issue_7e7797e4560f4f07b0b936d4dff032bb", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": "npc_029", "state_id": "todo", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Add partner sign-off conditions to CrownPeak duplicate-detection follow-up", "description": "Matthew Li approved a narrative dismissal for apinv_e0235997b4964a8f based on Brenda Abbas's vendor confirmation that VEN-030-353041 was a February 2026 CrownPeak platform-migration reissue and the matched paid sibling invoice ID VEN-030-353009 (paid 2026-03-13 for the March 2026 MSSP cycle).

Scope added by partner:

input.subject: Pre-sign-off verification — Brookfield May (FP-2026-05): legal accrual, Kestrel overcharge, duplicate AP item
input.content:
Daniel,

Before I sign the Brookfield May (FP-2026-05) management accounts, I had the three open support questions verified against source so we're not carrying working-paper estimates into the package. Nothing has been booked or changed — these are read-only findings for the sign-off file.

1) Outside-counsel legal-expense accrual (Lattimer & Crowe)
- George's working-paper estimate was $12,400 (engagement run-rate). His follow-up (10 Jun) corrected it: net out what L&C has already invoiced.
- L&C already has one invoice in AP — VEN-099-410022, $4,900, approved, posted 22 May to 530000 Professional Services - Legal.
- Correct incremental accrual to carry = $12,400 - $4,900 = $7,500.
- Watch point: the booked Q2-wrap accrual (JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0051, posted 1 Jun) debits 530000 for $52,185 / credits 220000 Accrued Professional Services, and its justification says it covers outside counsel + external advisors. We should confirm the L&C component inside that $52,185 is the $7,500 net figure, not the $12,400 gross - otherwise the $4,900 already sitting in AP/530000 gets double-counted.

2) Software-vendor overcharge (Kestrel Analytics)
- Contracted rate (Lena Park, signed order, 9 Jun): the period subscription is fixed at $7,900; anything billed above that is not contracted.
- SAP AP invoice VEN-098-220314 presents at $9,400 for "period subscription" (coded 520000).
- That's $1,500 above contract -> genuine overcharge, not a legitimate pass-through.
- For the accounts: it's still pending_approval and has NOT hit the GL (520000 shows no May Kestrel activity). Recommend holding/querying with the vendor and approving only the contracted $7,900 (dispute the $1,500) before it posts.

3) Possible duplicate AP bill
- Material/close-relevant duplicate: $6,068.91 on 131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions (exc_4d5d3582698946; BlackLine recon BL-7085321B04EA shows the exact -$6,068.91 variance). Confirmed real - the same annual software renewal posted twice: auto-renewal 15 May plus a manual re-key 22 May for the identical amount (Jones traced it; Ben confirmed it created two parallel amortization plans). Disposition already approved (reverse the 22 May manual re-key: DR 210000 / CR 131000 $6,068.91). Until that reversal is booked, prepaid + AP are overstated by $6,068.91 - it should be cleared before sign-off.
- Also surfaced in the raw SAP invoice detail: MetroShield Facility Services twin invoices - VEN-012-786680 and VEN-012-745157, both $145.14, consecutive SAP docs (5000000374/373), identical posting date, receipt timestamp, memo, GL (551000) and cost centre, both pending_approval. Looks like a double-key; immaterial but worth voiding one before approval.
- For clarity: Sean's open "duplicate deposit" is a bank-feed/cash exception (exc_3e229b67ef6147), separate from the AP bills above - it doesn't touch the AP/expense lines.

Net for sign-off: carry $7,500 on Lattimer & Crowe (and confirm the $52,185 accrual isn't grossing it up), hold Kestrel to the contracted $7,900, and get the $6,068.91 software-subscription reversal booked. Happy to talk before I sign.

Andrea

1) Legal accrual (Lattimer & Crowe): carry **$7,500 net** — the $12,400 working-paper estimate less the $4,900 L&C already invoiced and booked to 530000 (AP invoice VEN-099-410022, posted 22 May). Please confirm the $52,185 Q2-wrap professional-services accrual (JE-…-0051) reflects L&C at the $7,500 net, not the $12,400 gross, so we don't double-count the $4,900.

2) Software overcharge (Kestrel Analytics): contracted at **$7,900** (signed order, per Lena); SAP AP invoice VEN-098-220314 presents at **$9,400** for the period subscription → **$1,500 over contract = genuine overcharge**. Still pending approval, not yet in the GL — hold to the contracted $7,900 and query the $1,500 before it posts.

3) Duplicate AP bill: **$6,068.91 software-subscription renewal posted twice** (auto-renewal 15 May + manual re-key 22 May) on 131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions — exc_4d5d3582698946 / recon BL-7085321B04EA. Confirmed a real duplicate; reversal already approved (DR 210000 / CR 131000) and needs to be booked before sign-off. Separately spotted a tiny MetroShield twin-invoice pair in SAP ($145.14, VEN-012-786680 & -745157, both pending approval) — void one. (Note: Sean's "duplicate deposit" is a separate bank-feed/cash item, not these AP bills.)

Consolidated detail sent to Daniel.

content.text:
Done. I verified all three items against the source systems (Oracle GL, SAP subledger, BlackLine, plus George's and Lena's emails), sent Daniel a consolidated rundown, and posted a team summary in **#monthly-close-coordination**. Nothing was booked or changed — these are read-only findings for your sign-off.
